Accessibility Statement

CraneCheck is committed to ensuring digital accessibility for people with disabilities. We are continually improving the user experience for everyone and applying the relevant accessibility standards.

Conformance Status

We aim to conform to the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.1 at Level AA. These guidelines explain how to make web content more accessible to people with a wide array of disabilities.

Measures Taken

  • Skip navigation links for keyboard users
  • Visible focus indicators on all interactive elements
  • Proper heading hierarchy throughout all pages
  • ARIA landmarks and labels for screen reader navigation
  • Semantic HTML elements (nav, main, footer, article)
  • Form fields with associated labels and required field indicators
  • Sufficient color contrast ratios (4.5:1 minimum for text)
  • Reduced motion support for users who prefer less animation
  • Responsive design that works across all screen sizes
  • Alt text on all informative images

Feedback

We welcome your feedback on the accessibility of CraneCheck. If you encounter accessibility barriers, please let us know:

  • Email: support@cranecheck.com
  • Contact form: cranecheck.com/contact

We try to respond to accessibility feedback within 2 business days.

Compatibility

CraneCheck is designed to be compatible with the following assistive technologies:

  • Screen readers (VoiceOver, NVDA, JAWS)
  • Screen magnification software
  • Speech recognition software
  • Keyboard-only navigation

This statement was last updated on March 19, 2026.

CraneCheck

OSHA-compliant crane inspection software that keeps your crews safe and your records audit-ready.
